name: Schedule H — Homeowner/Renter Property Tax Credit
short_description: Property tax relief for low-to-moderate income DC residents who rent or own
long_description: Refundable credit based on property tax paid (or rent paid to landlord) compared to income. Often called the DC HRPTC. Can be claimed standalone.

name: Keep Child Care Affordable Credit
short_description: Refundable credit for child care expenses per eligible child
long_description: Capped at $1200 per eligible child for tax year 2025. Child must be born between Oct 1 2021 and Dec 31 2025 and claimed as a dependent.

name: DC Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: DC matches 100% of federal EITC for tax year 2025
long_description: For tax year 2025 DC EITC equals 100% of the federal EITC. Investment income must be under $11950.

name: Senior Citizen / Disabled Property Owner Tax Relief
short_description: 50% reduction in property tax for qualifying seniors or disabled homeowners
long_description: Reduces property tax by 50%. Owner must be 65+ or certified disabled with at least 50% ownership and reside in property.

name: Homestead Deduction
short_description: Deducts a fixed amount from property assessment for primary residence
long_description: Reduces taxable assessment of owner-occupied primary residence. Application required.

name: Refundable Virginia Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 20% of federal EITC refundable for full-year VA residents
long_description: Refundable credit equal to 20% of federal EITC (increased from 15% starting 2025). Cannot also claim CLI or non-refundable VA EITC.

name: Non-Refundable Virginia EITC
short_description: 20% of federal EITC as nonrefundable alternative
long_description: Non-refundable credit equal to 20% of federal EITC. Cannot exceed tax liability.

name: Credit for Low-Income Individuals (CLI)
short_description: $300 per personal/dependent exemption for VA filers below federal poverty
long_description: VAGI must be below federal poverty guidelines. Up to $300 per personal or dependent exemption.

name: Credit for Taxes Paid to Another State
short_description: Avoids double taxation when working across state lines
long_description: For VA residents whose income was also taxed by another state. Reciprocity with KY DC MD WV PA.

name: Livable Home Tax Credit
short_description: Credit for accessibility improvements to a primary residence
long_description: Up to $6500 for purchasing/retrofitting a residence with universal accessibility features.

name: Refundable Maryland EITC
short_description: Up to 45% of federal EITC for childless / 28% with children
long_description: MD residents who qualify for federal EITC also qualify. ITIN filers and childless adults under 25 also eligible.

name: Non-Refundable Maryland EITC
short_description: Equal to 50% of federal EITC applied first
long_description: Non-refundable component applied before refundable portion.

name: Maryland Poverty Level Credit
short_description: Eliminates state tax for filers below federal poverty
long_description: Eliminates state tax liability for taxpayers with income at or below federal poverty guidelines.

name: Maryland Child Tax Credit
short_description: Up to $500 per qualifying child for low-income families
long_description: State CTC up to $500 per qualifying child for families meeting income thresholds.

name: Homeowners' Property Tax Credit (HTC)
short_description: Property tax relief for homeowners with combined income under $60K
long_description: Combined gross household income cannot exceed $60000. Net worth excluding home/retirement under $200K.

name: Student Loan Debt Relief Tax Credit
short_description: Up to $5K for borrowers via competitive application
long_description: Competitive credit awarded by Maryland Higher Education Commission. Up to $5000 for qualifying student loan borrowers.

name: California Earned Income Tax Credit (CalEITC)
short_description: Up to $3756 for low-income working Californians
long_description: Earned income $1-$32900 to qualify. SSN or ITIN OK. Must live in CA more than half the year.

name: Young Child Tax Credit (YCTC)
short_description: $1189 per return for CalEITC families with child under 6
long_description: Must qualify for CalEITC and have qualifying child under 6 at end of tax year.

name: Foster Youth Tax Credit (FYTC)
short_description: $1189 per qualifying former foster youth
long_description: Ages 18-25 who were in CA foster care at age 13+. Up to $2378 if both spouses qualify.

name: California Renter's Credit
short_description: Nonrefundable credit for eligible CA renters
long_description: $60 single / $120 MFJ. Must have rented CA residence more than half the year.

name: California Child and Dependent Care Expenses Credit
short_description: State version of federal Child and Dependent Care Credit
long_description: Calculated under CA-specific rules and limitations.

name: NY State Earned Income Credit
short_description: 30% of federal EITC for NY residents
long_description: Refundable credit equal to 30% of federal EITC. Combined with federal can be worth thousands.

name: Empire State Child Credit
short_description: $1000/child under 4 or $330/child age 4-16
long_description: Substantially enhanced for 2025. Decoupled from federal CTC. Full-year NY residents only.

name: NY Real Property Tax Credit
short_description: Property tax relief for NY homeowners and renters
long_description: Refundable credit. Home market value under $85K. Renters: avg monthly rent $450 or less.

name: NY College Tuition Credit
short_description: Up to $400 per eligible student for tuition
long_description: For full-year NY residents who paid qualified college tuition.

name: NY Long-Term Care Insurance Credit
short_description: 20% of qualifying long-term care insurance premiums
long_description: Eligible for 20% of premiums paid for qualifying long-term care insurance policy.

name: Illinois Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 20% of federal EITC for IL residents
long_description: Refundable. ITIN filers and ages 18-24/65+ without dependents also eligible.

name: Illinois Child Tax Credit
short_description: 40% of IL EITC for families with child under 12
long_description: New for 2025 (increased from 20% to 40% of IL EITC).

name: Illinois Property Tax Credit
short_description: 5% of IL property tax paid on principal residence
long_description: Non-refundable. Income limits under $250K single / $500K MFJ.

name: Massachusetts EITC
short_description: 40% of federal EITC for MA residents
long_description: Refundable. One of highest state EITC matches at 40% of federal credit.

name: Massachusetts Child and Family Tax Credit
short_description: $440 per qualifying dependent (child under 13 or disabled/senior)
long_description: Refundable. $440 per qualifying dependent. No cap on number.

name: Senior Circuit Breaker Tax Credit
short_description: Property tax/rent relief for MA seniors 65+
long_description: Up to $2730 for seniors whose property tax (or 25% of rent) exceeds 10% of income.

name: Massachusetts Rental Deduction
short_description: Up to $4000 deduction for rent paid on MA primary residence
long_description: Deduction equal to 50% of rent paid up to $4000.

name: Washington Working Families Tax Credit
short_description: Modeled after federal EITC up to $1330
long_description: Up to $1330 depending on number of qualifying children. WA has no income tax. ITIN OK.

name: Colorado Earned Income Tax Credit (COEITC)
short_description: 50% of federal EITC for CO residents (TY 2025)
long_description: Up to ~50% of federal EITC for tax year 2025. ITIN filers and ages 18-24 also eligible.

name: Colorado Child Tax Credit (COCTC)
short_description: Up to $1200 per qualifying child under 6
long_description: 10-60% of federal CTC depending on income. For children under 6.

name: Colorado Family Affordability Tax Credit (FATC)
short_description: Up to $3200 per child under 6 / $2400 per child under 17
long_description: New credit for CO families. Phases out as income rises ($95K cutoff).

name: Colorado Property Tax/Rent/Heat Rebate
short_description: State rebate for seniors/disabled with limited income
long_description: Helps cover property tax rent and heating costs. 65+ or surviving spouse 58+ or disabled.

name: New Jersey Earned Income Tax Credit (NJEITC)
short_description: 40% of federal EITC for NJ residents
long_description: One of highest state EITC matches at 40%. Age floor lowered to 21+. ITIN filers eligible.

name: New Jersey Child Tax Credit
short_description: Up to $1000 per qualifying child under 6
long_description: Refundable credit for families earning $80K or less with child under 6.

name: New Jersey Property Tax Deduction/Credit
short_description: $50 credit OR deduction up to $15K of property tax paid
long_description: Choose deduction up to $15K of property taxes (or 18% of rent) OR a $50 refundable credit.

name: ANCHOR Property Tax Relief
short_description: Property tax/rent relief for homeowners and renters
long_description: Up to $1750 for homeowners 65+ ($1500 for under 65) and $700 for renters. Income-based.

name: Pennsylvania Tax Forgiveness Credit
short_description: Eliminates or reduces PA income tax for low-income filers
long_description: Reduces or eliminates PA personal income tax for eligible low-income filers. Family-size based.

name: Property Tax/Rent Rebate (PTRR)
short_description: Up to $1000 rebate for seniors/disabled on property tax or rent
long_description: Eligible: 65+ widows/widowers 50+ or disabled 18+. Income limit $48110.

name: Working Pennsylvanians Tax Credit (WPTC)
short_description: New refundable EITC at 10% of federal credit (TY 2026)
long_description: New for tax year 2026. Refundable credit at 10% of federal EITC.

name: Oregon Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 9% of federal EITC (12% if child under 3)
long_description: Refundable. 9% of federal EITC for most filers. Boosted to 12% for families with children under 3.

name: Oregon Kids' Credit
short_description: Up to $1000 per child under 5 for low-income families
long_description: Refundable credit for OR families with children under 5. Phases out around $30K income.

name: Oregon Working Family Household and Dependent Care Credit
short_description: Refundable credit for child/dependent care expenses
long_description: For working families with care expenses. Refundable. Income-based.

name: Minnesota Working Family Credit
short_description: ~37% of federal EITC for MN residents
long_description: State EITC equivalent at roughly 37% of federal credit. Refundable.

name: Minnesota Child Tax Credit
short_description: $1750 per qualifying child (most generous in US)
long_description: Most generous state CTC in the country at $1750 per qualifying child. Refundable.

name: Minnesota Property Tax Refund
short_description: Property tax refund for homeowners and renters
long_description: Up to $3500 for homeowners and ~$2570 for renters. Income-based.

name: Minnesota Renter's Property Tax Refund
short_description: Refund for renters whose rent funded property tax
long_description: Refundable. For MN renters whose 17% of rent (deemed property tax) exceeds threshold.

name: Georgia Low-Income Tax Credit
short_description: Tax credit for GA filers with federal AGI under $20K
long_description: Non-refundable credit. Federal AGI under $20000.

name: Georgia Child Tax Credit
short_description: $250 per child non-refundable (TY 2026)
long_description: New for tax year 2026. $250 per qualifying child.

name: Georgia Qualified Education Expense Credit
short_description: Credit for contributions to Student Scholarship Organizations
long_description: Donations to SSOs may be claimed as a credit. Pre-approval required.

name: North Carolina Credit for Children
short_description: Deduction up to $2500 per qualifying child
long_description: Deduction (not credit) up to $2500 per qualifying child for NC filers meeting income limits.

name: NC Property Tax Homestead Exclusion
short_description: Property tax exclusion for elderly/disabled
long_description: For NC residents 65+ or totally disabled. Excludes greater of $25K or 50% of home's value.

name: Michigan Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 30% of federal EITC for MI residents
long_description: Refundable. Increased from 6% to 30% in 2023.

name: Michigan Homestead Property Tax Credit
short_description: Property tax credit for owners and renters
long_description: Up to $1700. Total household resources under $71500. For MI homeowners and renters.

name: Michigan Home Heating Credit
short_description: Help with heating costs for low-income households
long_description: Refundable credit for MI residents to offset heating costs. Income-based.

name: Arizona Dependent Tax Credit
short_description: $100 per dependent under 17 / $25 per dependent 17+
long_description: Non-refundable. Phases out at higher incomes.

name: Arizona Property Tax Refund Credit
short_description: Refundable credit for AZ seniors with property tax
long_description: Up to $502. For AZ residents 65+ with limited income.

name: Arizona Charitable Tax Credit
short_description: Dollar-for-dollar credit for donations to qualifying charities
long_description: Up to $470 single / $938 MFJ for donations to Qualifying Charitable Organizations.

name: Ohio Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 30% of federal EITC (non-refundable) for OH residents
long_description: Non-refundable Ohio EITC at 30% of federal credit.

name: Ohio Child Tax Credit (proposed)
short_description: Up to $1000 per child under 6 (pending legislation)
long_description: Proposed; not yet enacted as of late 2025.

name: Ohio Homestead Exemption
short_description: Property tax reduction for elderly/disabled homeowners
long_description: Reduces property tax by exempting up to $26200 of home value. 65+ or permanently disabled.

name: Connecticut Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 40% of federal EITC + $250 for families with kids
long_description: Refundable. Connecticut EITC is 40% of federal + additional $250 for households with dependents.

name: Connecticut Property Tax Credit
short_description: Up to $300 credit for property tax on primary residence
long_description: Non-refundable. For CT homeowners. Income limits apply.

name: Connecticut Child Tax Rebate
short_description: $250 per child up to 3 children
long_description: Refundable rebate. Income limits.

name: Hawaii Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 40% of federal EITC for HI residents
long_description: Refundable Hawaii EITC at 40% of federal credit.

name: Hawaii Refundable Food/Excise Tax Credit
short_description: Up to $330 per exemption to offset food/excise tax
long_description: Refundable. Helps offset general excise tax on food. Per exemption claimed.

name: Hawaii Low-Income Household Renters Credit
short_description: $50 credit for low-income HI renters
long_description: For HI residents earning less than $30K who pay rent.

name: Indiana Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 10% of federal EITC for IN residents
long_description: Refundable Indiana EITC at 10% of federal credit.

name: Indiana Unified Tax Credit for the Elderly
short_description: Up to $140 for IN seniors with limited income
long_description: For IN residents 65+ with low federal AGI ($10K single / $19K MFJ).

name: Indiana Renter's Deduction
short_description: Up to $3000 deduction for IN renters
long_description: Deduct up to $3000 of rent paid on primary IN residence.

name: Kansas Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 17% of federal EITC for KS residents
long_description: Refundable Kansas EITC at 17% of federal credit.

name: Kansas Food Sales Tax Credit
short_description: Refundable credit for low-income KS residents to offset food tax
long_description: For KS residents 55+ or with dependents under 18 OR disabled. Income limit ~$30K.

name: Maine Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 50% of federal EITC childless / 25% with children
long_description: Refundable. Maine has highest match for childless workers at 50% of federal credit. ITIN filers eligible.

name: Maine Property Tax Fairness Credit
short_description: Up to $1500 property tax/rent relief for ME residents
long_description: Refundable. For ME homeowners and renters with limited income.

name: Maine Dependent Exemption Tax Credit
short_description: $300 credit per qualifying child under 17
long_description: Non-refundable credit per qualifying dependent. Phases out at higher incomes.

name: Missouri Working Family Credit
short_description: 10% of federal EITC (non-refundable) for MO residents
long_description: Non-refundable. Cannot exceed tax liability.

name: Missouri Property Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker)
short_description: Property tax/rent credit for MO seniors and disabled
long_description: Up to $1100 for homeowners or $750 for renters. 65+ or 100% disabled. Income limit ~$30K-$34K.

name: Montana Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 20% of federal EITC for MT residents (doubled in 2025)
long_description: Refundable. Doubled from 10% to 20% in 2025.

name: Montana Elderly Homeowner/Renter Credit
short_description: Up to $1150 for MT seniors with property tax/rent
long_description: Refundable. For MT residents 62+ with income under $45K.

name: Nebraska Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 10% of federal EITC for NE residents
long_description: Refundable Nebraska EITC at 10% of federal credit.

name: Nebraska Property Tax Credit
short_description: Refundable credit for school property taxes paid
long_description: For NE homeowners. Based on school district property tax paid.

name: New Mexico Working Families Tax Credit
short_description: 25% of federal EITC for NM residents
long_description: Refundable. NM Working Families Tax Credit at 25% of federal EITC. ITIN filers eligible.

name: New Mexico Low-Income Comprehensive Tax Rebate
short_description: Refundable rebate for low-income NM residents
long_description: Up to $730 depending on income and exemptions.

name: New Mexico Property Tax Rebate
short_description: For NM seniors with limited income
long_description: Refundable. For NM residents 65+ with income under $16K.

name: Oklahoma Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 5% of federal EITC for OK residents
long_description: Refundable Oklahoma EITC at 5% of federal credit.

name: Oklahoma Sales Tax Relief Credit
short_description: $40 per exemption to offset sales tax for low-income OK residents
long_description: Refundable. Income limit $20K single / $50K with dependents.

name: Rhode Island Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 16% of federal EITC for RI residents
long_description: Refundable. Increased from 15% in 2024 to 16% in 2025.

name: Rhode Island Property Tax Relief
short_description: Property tax/rent credit for RI seniors and disabled
long_description: Refundable. For RI residents 65+ or disabled with income under $35K.

name: South Carolina Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 125% of federal EITC (non-refundable) for SC residents
long_description: Non-refundable. Highest match in country at 125% but non-refundable so most filers get little benefit.

name: South Carolina Two Wage Earner Credit
short_description: $350 credit for married couples both working
long_description: Non-refundable. Helps reduce marriage penalty.

name: Utah Child Tax Credit
short_description: Up to $1000 per child under 5
long_description: Non-refundable. Increased eligible age to under 6 starting 2025. Income-based phase out.

name: Utah Retirement Tax Credit
short_description: Credit for UT retirees with limited income
long_description: Up to $450 per qualifying taxpayer 65+. Income limits apply.

name: Vermont Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 38% of federal EITC for VT (100% for childless)
long_description: Refundable. 38% with children / 100% for workers without children (raised 2025).

name: Vermont Child Tax Credit
short_description: $1000 per child under 7 for VT residents
long_description: Refundable. Expanded from under 6 to under 7 starting 2025. Phases out above $125K income.

name: Vermont Renter Credit
short_description: Renter's credit based on income and rent paid
long_description: Refundable. For VT renters with limited income.

name: West Virginia Low-Income Family Credit
short_description: Reduces WV income tax for very low-income filers
long_description: Non-refundable. Income limits depend on family size.

name: West Virginia Senior Citizen Tax Credit
short_description: Property tax credit for WV seniors
long_description: Refundable. For WV homeowners 65+.

name: Wisconsin Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 4-34% of federal EITC depending on # children
long_description: Refundable. 4% with 1 child / 11% with 2 / 34% with 3+ children. No credit for childless workers.

name: Wisconsin Homestead Credit
short_description: Property tax/rent credit for low-income WI residents
long_description: Refundable. Up to $1168 for WI homeowners and renters with income under $24680.

name: Iowa Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 15% of federal EITC for IA residents
long_description: Refundable Iowa EITC at 15% of federal credit.

name: Iowa Tuition and Textbook Credit
short_description: $500 per dependent for K-12 education expenses
long_description: Non-refundable. For IA parents paying K-12 education expenses.

name: Delaware Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: Choose 4.5% refundable OR 20% non-refundable
long_description: Delaware allows filers to choose between a 4.5% refundable credit or 20% non-refundable credit.

name: Kentucky Family Size Tax Credit
short_description: Reduces KY tax for low-income families
long_description: Non-refundable. Based on family size and federal poverty thresholds.

name: Louisiana Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: 5% of federal EITC for LA residents
long_description: Refundable Louisiana EITC at 5% of federal credit.

name: New Hampshire Education Credit
short_description: Limited credits as NH has no general income tax
long_description: NH does not have general income tax on wages. No EITC.

name: Mississippi (No state EITC)
short_description: MS does not currently offer state-level EITC
long_description: No state EITC. Standard federal credits only.

name: Arkansas (No state EITC)
short_description: AR does not offer state-level EITC
long_description: No state EITC. Property tax homestead credit available.

name: Alabama (No state EITC)
short_description: AL does not offer state-level EITC
long_description: No state EITC.

name: North Dakota Earned Income Tax Credit
short_description: Limited state credits — federal credits primary
long_description: ND has flat tax with limited state-level credits.

name: South Dakota (No income tax)
short_description: SD has no state income tax
long_description: No state EITC or income tax.

name: Alaska Permanent Fund Dividend
short_description: Annual dividend (not a tax credit) but unique to AK
long_description: Annual dividend paid to AK residents (not technically tax credit). $1700+ per resident in 2024.

name: Nevada (No income tax)
short_description: NV has no state income tax
long_description: No state EITC or income tax.

name: Tennessee (No income tax)
short_description: TN has no state income tax (Hall income tax repealed 2021)
long_description: No state EITC.

name: Wyoming (No income tax)
short_description: WY has no state income tax
long_description: No state EITC or income tax.

name: Idaho Grocery Credit
short_description: $135 per person to offset grocery sales tax
long_description: Non-refundable for most filers. ID is one of few states that taxes groceries.

name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
short_description: Federal credit for low-to-moderate income workers and families
long_description: Refundable federal credit. Maximum $8046 with 3+ qualifying children. Investment income limit $11950.

name: Child Tax Credit (CTC)
short_description: Per-child federal credit up to $1700 refundable as ACTC
long_description: Up to $2000 per qualifying child under 17. Refundable portion up to $1700.

name: American Opportunity Tax Credit (AOTC)
short_description: Up to $2500 per student for first 4 years of post-secondary education
long_description: 40% refundable (up to $1000). Half-time student in degree program.

name: Lifetime Learning Credit (LLC)
short_description: Up to $2000 per return for qualified education expenses
long_description: Non-refundable. No limit on years claimed.

name: Saver's Credit
short_description: Credit for contributions to retirement accounts
long_description: 10%/20%/50% of contributions up to $2000 ($4000 MFJ). Income-limited.

name: Premium Tax Credit
short_description: Helps offset health insurance premiums from Marketplace
long_description: Refundable. For coverage purchased through Health Insurance Marketplace.

name: Child and Dependent Care Credit
short_description: Credit for child care or dependent care expenses
long_description: Up to 35% of $3000 (1 dependent) or $6000 (2+) in care expenses.
